name: three-layer-session
description: Multi-turn agent session that separates immediate turn response, quick working-memory updates, and slower authoritative state consolidation.

goal: ship a three-layer memory runtime for local LLM agents
constraints: keep the synchronous turn path low latency
working memory may be approximate but must stay session scoped
stable state remains the authoritative long-term truth
decisions: We decide the fast layer should compile context from recent turns, retrieval, working memory, and stable state views
We decide working memory should update in the background without the full digest consistency gate
We decide stable state should only update through the controlled digest pipeline
todos: report fast path latency separately from stable-state latency
add replay checks for the authoritative state layer
openQuestions: Should working memory use a lightweight LLM extractor or stay heuristic by default?
risks: working memory could overfit to the most recent turn and drift before stable-state consolidation

goal: goal: wait for full digest completion before responding to users
goal: collapse all memory into one rolling summary
constraints: constraint: fast turns may directly overwrite stable state
decisions: We decide to remove the controlled digest pipeline and trust working memory directly

events.content: goal: ship a low-drift memory runtime for local LLM agents

constraint: keep the synchronous turn path low latency
constraint: stable state remains the authoritative long-term truth
We decide the fast layer should compile context from recent turns, retrieval, and state views
TODO: report fast path latency separately from digest latency
Status update: direct single-layer memory flow makes every turn too dependent on consolidation latency

goal: ship a three-layer memory runtime for local LLM agents
constraint: working memory may be approximate but must stay session scoped

We decide stable state should only update through the controlled digest pipeline
TODO: add replay checks for the authoritative state layer
Status update: runtime now returns immediately and lets background jobs refresh working memory and stable state separately

What constraints still apply to the runtime?
What did we decide about working memory and stable state?
